commit.message:
feat: agenda system, bug fix (#123)

* feat: topic agenda system with concept-based onboarding

Replace flat skill selection with a structured topic agenda that
supports role-based concept browsing, prerequisite-aware ordering,
and configurable assessment thoroughness (quick/standard/thorough).

Backend:
- Add AgendaItem, TopicStatus, Thoroughness state types
- Add build_agenda pipeline node between calibration and assessment
- Add GET /roles/{role_id}/concepts endpoint with topo-sorted concepts
- Add display_name field to all knowledge base YAML concepts
- Infer confidence for un-assessed concepts from assessed prerequisites
- Dynamic question budget based on agenda size and thoroughness
- Conclude assessment when no pending topics remain

Frontend:
- Replace SkillBrowser with ConceptBrowser grouped by level
- Fetch concepts per role/level via new API endpoint
- Add max 10 concept selection limit with disabled state
- Show estimated question count from thoroughness setting

When roleId is provided, the route now calls get_target_graph_for_concepts
instead of get_target_graph. Tests without roleId also need get_all_topics
mocked for the new estimated_questions computation.

* fix: auto-create openlearning_test DB on postgres container init

Mount an init script in /docker-entrypoint-initdb.d/ so the test
database is created alongside the main database on first container
startup. Prevents local test failures after volume recreation.

   only create new nodes for unassessed prerequisites

commit.message:
Fix movement: use frame-step walk model for wall-safe movement

Replace target-tile model with a step-based loop that consumes movement budget
each frame. Direction changes and wall checks only happen when exactly at a tile
center (integer snap point), making it physically impossible to enter a wall.

feat: add Vitest test environment and CI test job (#21)

No automated tests or test infrastructure existed; CI only ran type
checks and builds.

## Changes

- **`vitest.config.ts`** — new Vitest config with `jsdom` environment
for browser API access (`localStorage`, `Notification`)
- **`src/storage.test.ts`** — 16 unit tests covering all CRUD functions
including `notified` reset logic in `updateProduct`
- **`src/notifications.test.ts`** — 9 unit tests for
`getDaysUntilExpiry` (boundary cases) and `checkAndNotify` (permission
guard, threshold logic, deduplication via `notified` flag)
- **`package.json`** — adds `"test": "vitest run"` script
- **`tsconfig.json`** — includes `vitest.config.ts` in compilation scope
- **`.github/workflows/ci.yml`** — adds `test` job running `npm test`
alongside existing `typecheck` and `build` jobs

Module mocking uses `vi.mock` hoisted at the top level; `Notification`
global is stubbed per-test via `vi.stubGlobal`:

```ts
vi.mock('./storage.ts', () => ({
  loadProducts: vi.fn(),
  markNotified: vi.fn(),
}));

beforeEach(() => {
  const MockNotification = vi.fn();
  Object.defineProperty(MockNotification, 'permission', { value: 'granted', writable: true, configurable: true });
  vi.stubGlobal('Notification', MockNotification);
});
```

commit.message:
fix: edit modal save closes window and shows save feedback (#20)

The save button in the edit modal had no error boundary — if
`updateProduct` or `renderProductList` threw (e.g. localStorage quota
exceeded), `cleanup()` was never reached and the modal stayed open.
There was also no confirmation that the save succeeded.

## Changes

- **`showToast(message, type)`** — new helper that appends a temporary
`role="status"` / `aria-live="polite"` div at the top of the screen,
fades out after 3 s
- **`onSave` wrapped in try/catch:**
- Success → `cleanup()` closes modal, then green `✅ Produkt gespeichert`
toast
- Error → inline `edit-error-msg` shows `"Fehler beim Speichern. Bitte
versuche es erneut."`, modal stays open for retry

```ts
try {
  updateProduct(product.id, { name, expiryDate, notifyDaysBefore });
  renderProductList(container);
  checkAndNotify();
  cleanup();
  showToast('✅ Produkt gespeichert');
} catch {
  if (errorMsg) errorMsg.textContent = 'Fehler beim Speichern. Bitte versuche es erneut.';
}
```

## Screenshot

![Modal open before
save](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/14683926-a0ae-4c6a-bfa2-729bd1a79b5d)
![Modal closed with success
toast](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/7f085375-e2f1-4f06-bef8-ce18b86dd2e1)

Pac-Man and ghosts now move tile-to-tile toward explicit target tile centers.
Wall checks happen before setting the next target tile, so it is impossible
for either entity to enter a wall tile.

commit.message:
Add Pac-Man game

Complete client-side Pac-Man game built with HTML5 Canvas, CSS3, and vanilla JavaScript.

Features:
- 60FPS game loop with requestAnimationFrame
- 4 ghosts with BFS pathfinding (Chase/Scatter/Frightened/Eaten modes)
- Power pellets, dot collection, lives system
- Scoring: dots=10, pellets=50, ghosts=200/400/800/1600
- Web Audio API sound effects (no external files)
- Keyboard (Arrow/WASD) + touch swipe + D-pad controls
- Mobile responsive canvas scaling
- 2 levels with increasing difficulty

commit.message:
feat(orchestrator): add configurable drain timeout to circuit breaker

Add circuit_breaker_drain_timeout config (default 900s/15min) that limits how
long the drain phase waits for remaining agents after the circuit breaker trips.
When timeout is exceeded, remaining agents are forcibly terminated via
_drain_orphaned_futures. Setting to 0 disables the timeout (wait forever).

Fixes: Circuit breaker drain phase had no timeout, causing stuck agents to block
graceful shutdown indefinitely (observed 70-minute stall in run 20260324_172150).

commit.message:
Add POSIX keystring parsing and argv pre-expansion (#114)

Support traditional key syntax (for example `tar cvf archive.tar file`)
by rewriting a leading key operand into clap-compatible short options
before argument parsing.

- detect valid keystrings in argv[1]
- expand key letters into `-<opt>` arguments
- consume operands for `f` and `b` in key order
- forward `-b` (currently unsupported) so key/dash forms fail consistently
- update usage text to document key syntax
- add unit tests for key detection/expansion
- add integration tests for create/extract parity and `-b` failure parity

commit.message:
feat(api): add /api/arrivals/route endpoint matching MCP logic (#46)

The `route_arrivals` MCP tool's `display_url` pointed to
`/api/arrivals/nearby`, which just does a spatial proximity search. The
actual tool computes origin→destination route matching (sequence-aware
direct routes + pgRouting transfers)—so the mobile app was rendering
incorrect results on refresh.

### Changes

- **New endpoint `GET /api/arrivals/route`**
(`internal/api/arrivals_route.go`): Accepts `lat`, `lon`, `dest_lat`,
`dest_lon`, `radius_origin`, `radius_dest`, `max_transfers`. Calls
`RouteArrivalsService.Execute()` and converts the result into the
`{stops: [...]}` format expected by `ArrivalCard`.
- **Updated `display_url`** in `internal/tools/tools.go`:
`route_arrivals` now emits `/api/arrivals/route?lat=…&dest_lat=…`
instead of `/api/arrivals/nearby?lat=…&routes=…`.
- **Registered endpoint** in `cmd/server/main.go`.

```
# Before (wrong — loses route-planning context)
/api/arrivals/nearby?lat=22.28&lon=114.15&radius=300&routes=1A%2C960

# After (same logic as MCP tool)
/api/arrivals/route?lat=22.28&lon=114.15&dest_lat=22.32&dest_lon=114.17&radius_origin=300&radius_dest=300&max_transfers=2
```

the mcp tool returned bus

commit.message:
Fix ClassDecoratorSetting and get_class_settings to correctly handle __databind_settings__ on subclasses (#77)

`ClassDecoratorSetting.__call__` used `getattr()` to look up
`__databind_settings__`, which resolves through MRO and returns the
parent's list when the subclass has none. It then appends to that parent
list in-place — but `get_class_settings()` originally used `vars()`,
which only checks the class's own `__dict__`, so the setting was never
found on the subclass. Net effect: `@ExtraKeys()` on a subclass whose
parent was already decorated was silently a no-op.

Additionally, `get_class_settings()` did not traverse the MRO, meaning
subclasses could not inherit settings (such as `ExtraKeys()`) from
parent classes at all.

## Changes

- **`settings.py` — `ClassDecoratorSetting.__call__`**: Replace
`getattr(type_, "__databind_settings__", None)` with
`vars(type_).get("__databind_settings__", None)`, ensuring a new list is
always created on the target class rather than reusing an inherited one.

- **`settings.py` — `get_class_settings`**: Updated to traverse
`type_.__mro__` so that subclasses inherit `ClassDecoratorSetting`
settings (e.g. `ExtraKeys()`) from parent classes. The child class's own
settings are yielded first, so a child can override a parent's setting
by decorating itself explicitly (e.g. `@ExtraKeys(allow=False)`).

- **`converters_test.py`**: Add three regression tests covering the
scenario:
- Both parent and child decorated with `@ExtraKeys()` each get
independent `__databind_settings__` lists, and the parent's list is not
polluted.
- A decorated subclass correctly allows extra keys during
deserialization.
- An undecorated subclass inherits `ExtraKeys()` from its parent (extra
keys allowed), while a child explicitly decorated with
`@ExtraKeys(allow=False)` overrides that and rejects extra keys.

```python
@ExtraKeys()
@dataclasses.dataclass
class Parent:
    a: int

@dataclasses.dataclass
class Child(Parent):  # Inherits ExtraKeys() from Parent via MRO
    b: str = ""

mapper.deserialize({"a": 1, "b": "hello", "extra": "ignored"}, Child)  # Now works correctly

@ExtraKeys(allow=False)
@dataclasses.dataclass
class ChildOverriding(Parent):  # Overrides parent's ExtraKeys()
    b: str = ""

mapper.deserialize({"a": 1, "b": "hello", "extra": "ignored"}, ChildOverriding)  # Raises ConversionError
```

no own `__databind_settings__`, the decorator's `getattr()` call falls
back to the parent's list via MRO traversal and appends to it in place,
rather than creating a new list on the subclass. As a result,
`get_class_settings()`, which uses `vars()`, finds no setting on the
subclass, making `ExtraKeys` effectively a no-op for it. The bug is
order-dependent: it only triggers when `ExtraKeys()` is applied to a
